# Guided Discovery

## Intent

Teach the concept first, then use the simulation to confirm and deepen understanding before practice and reflection.

## Best For

- Concepts that need context before an interaction becomes meaningful
- Lessons where learners benefit from explanation-first scaffolding
- STEM sections with a strong glossary or vocabulary support need

## Not Ideal For

- Purely exploratory lessons that should start cold with no setup
- Visual-first topics where a diagram or figure should dominate the opening

## Learner Fit

- General
- Scaffolded
- Analytical

## Subjects

- Mathematics
- Physics
- Biology
- Chemistry

## Lesson Flow

Hook -> Explain -> Define -> Simulate -> Example -> Practice -> Reflect -> What Next

## Required Components

- SectionHeader
- HookHero
- ExplanationBlock
- SimulationBlock
- PracticeStack
- WhatNextBridge

## Optional Components

- DefinitionCard
- WorkedExampleCard
- PitfallAlert
- GlossaryRail
- DiagramBlock
- ReflectionPrompt

## Why This Template Exists

Not every lesson benefits from dropping learners straight into an interactive. Guided Discovery gives enough explanation for the learner to form a mental model, then uses the simulation to verify and strengthen it.

## Responsive Notes

On desktop, keep the main lesson flow alongside the glossary support. Below tablet, collapse the sidebar behavior and keep the simulation full width inside the main reading column.

## Print Notes

Flatten the glossary support into the lesson flow, expand step-based content, and replace the simulation with its fallback diagram.
